commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b6d872e172ce0ef38153da1d7e1265f079df9acc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR hoists the interactive session-store lookup, passes its sessionId into the dispatch diagnostic lifecycle, and adds a Slack interactive regression test.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +33. Total +34 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main creates the interactive diagnostic lifecycle with sessionKey only, and the introduced native-target mismatch is also source-reproducible from the target-aware lookup; I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor proof and a small session-ref correction before normal maintainer review can continue; automation cannot supply the contributor's real environment proof.

target.title: fix(diagnostics): carry session UUID on interactive dispatch events
target.body:
## Why

Plugins and exporters that subscribe to diagnostic events via `onDiagnosticEvent` need the canonical session UUID — the same id OpenClaw's JSONL session transcript writes as its session-header `id` field (`src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/bootstrap.ts`) — so downstream systems can join diagnostic/telemetry rows back to the transcript by id.

The **cron / isolated-agent** path already provides it: `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts` passes `sessionId: prepared.context.runSessionId` into `createDiagnosticMessageLifecycle`, so `message.queued` and `session.state` events carry the UUID.

The **interactive dispatch** path does not. `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` creates the diagnostic lifecycle with `sessionKey` only — even though the canonical UUID is available from the session store entry (`resolveSessionStoreLookup(ctx, cfg).entry?.sessionId`, the same source already used a few lines later to resolve `operationSessionId`). As a result every interactive `message.queued` / `session.state` event emits `sessionId: undefined`, and consumers cannot join those events to the transcript by UUID.

This is an asymmetry/oversight, not a design choice — there is no config that turns it on.

## Change

Pass `sessionId: initialSessionStoreEntry.entry?.sessionId` into the interactive `createDiagnosticMessageLifecycle({...})` call. The `resolveSessionStoreLookup(ctx, cfg)` lookup (read-only; needs only `ctx` + `cfg`, both already in scope) is hoisted a few lines up so its result is available at lifecycle-creation time. The lifecycle's existing `resolveRef` then defaults `sessionId` onto every emitted event (`message.queued`, `session.state`, `message.processed`), matching the cron path.

Safety: when the store entry exists, `entry?.sessionId` equals the UUID written to the JSONL session header. When it is absent (brand-new session not yet persisted), it is `undefined`, which preserves today's `sessionKey`-only behavior — the change never emits a *mismatched* UUID. This mirrors the existing canonical resolution (`operationSessionId = ... ?? initialSessionStoreEntry.entry?.sessionId ?? ...`).

## Files

- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` — hoist `initialSessionStoreEntry` lookup above the lifecycle creation; pass `sessionId` (+2/−1).
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` — new test asserting interactive diagnostic events carry the session-store UUID.

## Tests

New test `carries the session store UUID on interactive diagnostic events`: drives a real interactive (Slack) dispatch with a store entry whose `sessionId` is `test-uuid-1234`, and asserts the emitted `logMessageQueued` and `logSessionStateChange` both carry `sessionId: "test-uuid-1234"`. Red before the change, green after. Full `dispatch-from-config.test.ts` suite remains green.

## Scope

Independent of #80497 (which adds `onModelDiagnosticEvent` at the delivery/filter layer in `src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts`). This PR is at the emit/population layer (`dispatch-from-config.ts`) and touches disjoint files; the two can land in either order.
target.author: arniesaha
